rationale: Goodman and Irmak (2013) is cited in the theory development sections, not just in a background list. The citation supports key premises used to derive H2 and H4, including errors in usage-frequency estimation and the prepurchase having-versus-using mindset. The citing paper then tests a new antecedent (experience variety) of perceived usage frequency, so the cited work functions as a conceptual base for the mechanism.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Goodman and Irmak (2013) as core prior evidence that consumers often fail to estimate how much they will use products and that usage-frequency beliefs shape evaluation. It builds on this foundation to propose and test that perceived experience variety shifts frequency judgments and thereby affects postpurchase outcomes.
